description: Sensor layer definitions for monitoring mandala computation. Each sensor reads a specific observable from the computation state and emits structured data.

sensors.description: Total system energy: sum of cell energies and coupling energies.
sensors.interpretation: Lower is better. Ground state = solution found.

sensors.description: Energy change from most recent state transition.
sensors.interpretation: Negative = system relaxing toward solution. Near-zero = converged.

sensors.description: Current temperature controlling exploration vs exploitation.
sensors.interpretation: High T = exploration. Low T = exploitation. Annealing decreases T over time.

sensors.description: Rate of energy decrease over recent relaxation steps.
sensors.interpretation: Approaching zero = system is near equilibrium.

sensors.description: Distribution of octahedral states across all cells.

sensors.source: Cell neighbor lists + FRET coupling strengths
sensors.description: Weighted graph of inter-cell coupling strengths.
sensors.interpretation: Strong edges = tightly coupled subsystems. Clusters = problem structure.

sensors.description: Overlap between current quantum state and target ground state.
sensors.interpretation: 1.0 = perfect solution. Only available in quantum mode.

sensors.source: Von Neumann entropy of reduced density matrix
sensors.interpretation: High = cells are quantum-correlated. Relevant for quantum advantage.

layers.description: Sensors are organized into layers for different monitoring granularities.

emit_format.description: Standardized output format for sensor readings.
